name: First Community Bank
description_short: South Carolina-chartered community bank with 20+ locations across SC and northeast Georgia, offering personal and business banking, mortgages, and financial consulting services since 1995.
description_long:
First Community Bank was founded in 1995 by Jim Leventis and Mike Crapps with the mission to provide local business owners with a better financial partner invested in long-term growth. Starting with two locations in Lexington and Forest Acres, South Carolina, the bank has grown to over 20 locations across South Carolina and northeast Georgia with approximately 250 employees. The bank has expanded through several strategic mergers, including acquisitions of Cornerstone National Bank (2017), Savannah River Banking Company (2014), Bank of Camden (2006), and Newberry Federal Savings Bank (2004). The bank was publicly traded on the NASDAQ SmallCap Market as First Community Corporation (FCCO) beginning in 2003.

First Community Bank offers a comprehensive range of banking services for both personal and business customers. Their service portfolio includes online banking, mobile banking apps for Android and iOS, checking and savings accounts, business banking solutions through their Business+ platform, mortgage services (they acquired Palmetto South Mortgage in 2011), and loan products. The bank also provides financial consulting services through First Community Financial Consultants (established 2008) and offers staff of financial experts and business bankers to guide customers' financial strategies. They maintain an active customer support team accessible at 800.829.6372 and have a specific team member program allowing customers to meet with dedicated bankers.

First Community Bank distinguishes itself through its explicit community banking philosophy and personalized service model. The company emphasizes direct access to real people rather than automated systems, and their leadership highlights values of integrity, excellence, and service. They maintain a local presence across their service regions and have developed specialized roles like Financial Consultants (Jason A. Langdale holds the CFP® designation). The bank recently welcomed Signature Bank customers into their family, indicating recent merger or acquisition activity. They also demonstrate commitment to customer education through fraud prevention resources and provide resources for PPP loan customers.

As a regional community bank, First Community Bank is best suited for small to mid-sized businesses and personal banking customers in South Carolina and northeast Georgia who prioritize local relationships and personalized service. The main limitation is geographic restriction to SC and northeast GA, making them inaccessible to customers outside these regions. While they offer modern digital banking (mobile apps, online banking, Business+ platform), specific details about product rates, fees, and competitive offerings are not disclosed on their public website. Customers seeking comprehensive financial services (lending, consulting, mortgages) in their service area will find this bank well-positioned, but those in other regions must look elsewhere. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Business+ online banking platform for business customers
Mortgage lending (home purchase, refinance, and related products)
Financial consulting services through First Community Financial Consultants
Fraud education and customer protection resources
pros: 30+ years of local community banking experience with established track record of growth from 2 to 20+ locations
Personalized service model with direct access to real people and dedicated banker relationships rather than automated systems
Comprehensive service offerings including personal/business banking, mortgages, loan products, and financial consulting with CFP® professionals
Multiple modern digital banking channels (online banking, mobile apps for iOS and Android, Business+ platform) alongside in-person service
FDIC-insured member bank with South Carolina charter, providing regulatory safety and credibility
Strategic merger activity showing financial stability (recent Signature Bank integration, multiple past acquisitions)
Fraud education resources and customer protection programs (PPP scam awareness materials, security prioritization)
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to South Carolina and northeast Georgia only, unavailable for customers outside these regions
Website lacks specific information on account features, interest rates, fees, minimum balance requirements, or product-specific terms
No transparent comparison of rates or features relative to other banks or pricing details for loans and mortgages
Limited online presence and digital marketing compared to larger regional or national banks; minimal social proof or customer reviews visible
Website enrollment link for online banking is non-functional (returns application error at https://b053202208.flex.online-banking-services.com)
best_for: Small to mid-sized business owners in South Carolina or northeast Georgia seeking personalized banking relationships with dedicated business bankers
Personal banking customers in SC/GA service areas prioritizing local community bank service and face-to-face relationships over digital-only banking
Homebuyers in their service regions seeking mortgage services with local lending expertise and personalized consultant support
Customers requiring comprehensive financial planning services combined with banking, such as business owners needing long-term growth guidance
diagnosis: First Community Bank is best for small-to-mid-sized business owners and personal banking customers located in South Carolina or northeast Georgia who value personalized, relationship-based banking from a locally-rooted community bank with 30+ years of experience. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—the bank only serves SC and parts of northeast GA, making it inaccessible outside these regions, and detailed product rates, fees, and terms are not publicly available online.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
